# UC-22.16.3 · Lateral authentication chains across OT VLANs

## Description

Supports pipeline cybersecurity program oversight under TSA Pipeline Security Directive by correlating operational telemetry with governance records for "Lateral authentication chains across OT VLANs".

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for TSA SD compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes wineventlog and sourcetypes WinEventLog:Security. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures. Indexes required: `index=wineventlog`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Edge Hub, Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)..
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `pan:traffic`, SCADA/HMI audit, PAM, `nozomi:alert`, NetFlow, ITSM, passive OT asset discovery.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=wineventlog sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security earliest=-24h EventCode=4624 Logon_Type=3
| eval uc_tag="22.16.3"
| lookup pipeline_ot_hosts.csv ComputerName OUTPUT ot_zone
| where isnotnull(ot_zone)
| transaction src_ip maxspan=300s maxpause=30s
| eval hops=eventcount
| where hops>12
| table src_ip, hops, duration, ot_zone
```

Understanding this SPL

**Lateral authentication chains across OT VLANs (TSA IR readiness)** — Supports pipeline cybersecurity program oversight under TSA Pipeline Security Directive by correlating operational telemetry with governance records for "Lateral authentication chains across OT VLANs".

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:traffic`, SCADA/HMI audit, PAM, `nozomi:alert`, NetFlow, ITSM, passive OT asset discovery. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Edge Hub, Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: wineventlog; **sourcetype**: WinEventLog:Security. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=wineventlog, sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_tag** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(ot_zone)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Groups related events into transactions — prefer `maxspan`/`maxpause`/`maxevents` for bounded memory.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **hops** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where hops>12` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Lateral authentication chains across OT VLANs (TSA IR readiness)** — Supports pipeline cybersecurity program oversight under TSA Pipeline Security Directive by correlating operational telemetry with governance records for "Lateral authentication chains across OT VLANs".

Documented **Data sources**: `pan:traffic`, SCADA/HMI audit, PAM, `nozomi:alert`, NetFlow, ITSM, passive OT asset discovery. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Edge Hub, Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Network_Traffic.All_Traffic` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: duration, hops, ot_zone, src_ip, uc_tag.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP or perimeter firewall log (for example Palo Alto or FortiGate) for the same window. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Choropleth or site bar, Table, Single value

## SPL

```spl
index=wineventlog sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security earliest=-24h EventCode=4624 Logon_Type=3
| eval uc_tag="22.16.3"
| lookup pipeline_ot_hosts.csv ComputerName OUTPUT ot_zone
| where isnotnull(ot_zone)
| transaction src_ip maxspan=300s maxpause=30s
| eval hops=eventcount
| where hops>12
| table src_ip, hops, duration, ot_zone
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Time chart, Choropleth or site bar, Table, Single value. Key fields: `duration`, `hops`, `ot_zone`, `src_ip`, `uc_tag`.
